plan.title: Post-Alignment Cloud GPU Training Execution Plan

doc.notes: 6 systemic issues — all code-fixed, regression tests pass

29 hypotheses. H8 (git clone), H14 (Python 3.13), H22 (uv sync), H27 (outdated MONAI) are IRRELEVANT with Docker image_id. Valid: H13 (splits), H15 (env vars), H17 (boto3 CRC32C), H20 (VesselFM cache), H23 (EXPERIMENT in envs).

CONTAINS BANNED CONTENT: D2 chose 'Native execution (uv sync + direct training)'. <no_docker_registry_rationale> section explicitly defends bare VM. Cost model includes uv install overhead. ALL WRONG. Valid: D1 (SkyPilot managed jobs), D3 (Hydra config), D4 (MLflow cloud).

T1.1 describes bare VM setup (git clone + uv sync) — BANNED. RC8 ('RunPod images lack uv') — IRRELEVANT with Docker. RC12 (MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1) — STILL CORRECT. Test suite structure (L1-L4) — VALID, being implemented.
4-layer composable test suite — implementation in progress
Phase 0 done. Phases 1-4 included in this plan.
Research report — architectural decisions still valid.
6 decisions (D1-D6) — all phases included in this plan.
Pulumi stack deployed, MLflow server running.

hypothesis.title: Git clone fails on RunPod — wrong branch
hypothesis.reason: No git clone needed — code baked into Docker image

hypothesis.title: Python 3.13 not available on RunPod VM
hypothesis.reason: Python version controlled by Docker image, not VM

hypothesis.title: uv sync fails to compile C extensions on RunPod
hypothesis.reason: All deps pre-compiled in Docker image build stage

hypothesis.title: Outdated MONAI on RunPod base image
hypothesis.reason: MONAI version controlled by Docker image, not VM

hypothesis.title: RunPod base images do NOT include uv
hypothesis.reason: Our Docker image includes uv — RunPod base image is irrelevant

hypothesis.title: Setup: git clone, uv sync, install deps
hypothesis.reason: Setup is DATA ONLY — DVC pull + config copy. No dep installation.

phase.name: Docker Image Build + Push
phase.description: Rebuild minivess-base with latest code and push to GHCR with git SHA tag. This is the ONLY blocker — everything else is ready.

task.issue_title: chore(docker): rebuild minivess-base + push to GHCR with git SHA tag
task.description:
1. Rebuild: make build-base-gpu (includes latest code changes) 2. Tag with git SHA: docker tag minivess-base:latest ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:$(git rev-parse --short HEAD) 3. Push both :latest and :sha tags to GHCR 4. Verify: docker manifest inspect ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest
The .env sourcing issue with GHCR_USERNAME needs fixing — grep-based extraction works but `source .env` does not (variable ordering or export issue).

tdd_spec.green: - Run make build-base-gpu - Run make push-ghcr (fix .env sourcing in Makefile if needed) - Verify with docker manifest inspect

phase.name: P0 Smoke Tests — SAM3 + VesselFM
phase.description:
Launch smoke tests for SAM3 (vanilla + hybrid) and VesselFM on RunPod. These are P0 because they CANNOT be finetuned locally (require >8GB VRAM). Cost: ~$0.36 total for all 3 models.
SAM3 vanilla: 2.9 GB inference → fits RTX 4090 easily SAM3 hybrid: 7.18 GiB at patch 64x64x3 → needs patch override on 8GB, fine on 24GB VesselFM: ~6 GB inference, ~10 GB training → fits RTX 4090

task.issue_title: test(cloud): launch sam3_vanilla smoke test on RunPod
task.description:
make smoke-test-preflight # Validate env vars make smoke-test-gpu MODEL=sam3_vanilla # Launch on RunPod (~$0.06, ~5 min) make verify-smoke-test MODEL=sam3_vanilla # Verify on cloud MLflow
Expected: 2 epochs, 1 fold, 4 volumes. Training completes, metrics logged to UpCloud MLflow at 185.20.139.158:5000.

task.issue_title: test(cloud): launch sam3_hybrid smoke test on RunPod
task.description:
make smoke-test-gpu MODEL=sam3_hybrid
NOTE: sam3_hybrid on RTX 4090 (24GB) should work with default patch size (64,64,3). The patch override to (32,32,3) was only needed for 8GB GPU (RTX 2070 Super). If OOM occurs, add HYDRA_OVERRIDES with smaller patch size.

task.issue_title: test(cloud): launch vesselfm smoke test on RunPod
task.description:
make smoke-test-gpu MODEL=vesselfm
NOTE: VesselFM requires HF_TOKEN for weight download. The smoke_test_gpu.yaml setup section pre-caches weights during setup (H20) to avoid burning GPU time. HF_TOKEN is confirmed set in .env.

task.issue_title: test(cloud): verify all 3 smoke test results on cloud MLflow
task.description:
For each model: make verify-smoke-test MODEL={model}
Verify: 1. MLflow experiment exists on cloud server 2. Run status is FINISHED (not FAILED/KILLED) 3. Metrics logged (train_loss, val_loss if validation ran) 4. Tags: flow_name, MODEL_FAMILY, sys_gpu_model 5. Artifacts: hydra config, checkpoints

phase.name: Delete Bare-VM SkyPilot YAMLs
phase.description: train_generic.yaml and train_hpo_sweep.yaml use bare-VM setup (pip install uv, uv sync). This violates the Docker mandate. Delete them. They will be recreated from scratch when full training (not smoke tests) is needed, using the Docker image_id pattern from smoke_test_gpu.yaml as template.

task.description:
Delete: - deployment/skypilot/train_generic.yaml (bare VM: pip install uv, uv sync) - deployment/skypilot/train_hpo_sweep.yaml (bare VM: pip install uv, uv sync)
Update: - Makefile: remove any references to these files - CLAUDE.md: update SkyPilot section if it references these - Tests: remove or update any tests that reference these files

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_skypilot_bare_vm_ban.py::test_no_bare_vm_skypilot_yamls → Glob deployment/skypilot/*.yaml, verify NONE contain "pip install" or "uv sync" in setup: section
tdd_spec.green: - Delete the two files - Update references

phase.name: P1 Model Expansion
phase.description:
Extend smoke tests to ALL ModelAdapter implementations. These are P1 because they CAN be finetuned locally (fit in 8GB VRAM).
Known adapters: DynUNet, SegResNet (from model_profiles/*.yaml) Future adapters: CoMMA Mamba, U-Like Mamba (planned)
Each model needs a smoke test experiment config in configs/experiment/smoke_*.yaml and must work with the existing smoke_test_gpu.yaml via MODEL_FAMILY env var.

task.issue_title: feat(skypilot): smoke test configs for all ModelAdapter models
task.description:
For each registered ModelAdapter: 1. Ensure configs/experiment/smoke_{model}.yaml exists 2. Ensure model works with the smoke_test_gpu.yaml flow 3. Add to make smoke-test-all target 4. Document VRAM requirements and expected cost
Models to verify: dynunet, segresnet, and any other registered adapters.

tdd_spec.red: - tests/v2/unit/test_smoke_configs_complete.py::test_every_model_has_smoke_config → For each model in model_profiles/*.yaml, verify matching smoke_*.yaml exists - test_smoke_configs_reference_valid_models → Each smoke config's model field matches a registered ModelAdapter
tdd_spec.green: - Create missing smoke_*.yaml experiment configs - Update Makefile smoke-test-all to include all models

task.issue_title: test(cloud): launch P1 model smoke tests on RunPod
task.description: For each P1 model: make smoke-test-gpu MODEL={model} Verify results: make verify-smoke-test MODEL={model}

phase.name: Production Training YAMLs (Replace Deleted)
phase.description: Create new SkyPilot YAMLs for full training and HPO using Docker image_id. These replace the deleted bare-VM versions. They use the same Docker image as smoke tests but with full training parameters (100 epochs, 3 folds, etc.).

task.description: New SkyPilot YAML for production training: - image_id: docker:ghcr.io/petteriteikari/minivess-base:latest - Multi-cloud failover: RunPod → AWS → GCP → Lambda - Spot with automatic recovery - Full training: 100 epochs, 3 folds, cbdice_cldice loss - Setup: DATA ONLY (same pattern as smoke_test_gpu.yaml) - BANNED: apt-get, uv sync, git clone, pip install in setup

stream.name: Profiler and Benchmarking
stream.description:
Full profiling infrastructure as core infrastructure (default ON). Imported from profiler-benchmarking-plan-execution.xml (reviewed, round 2). Phase 0 config is DONE. Phases 1-4 remain.
Architecture: trainer.py ──record_function──> torch.profiler ──> MLflow artifacts trainer.py ──epoch_summary──> system_monitor ──> MLflow metrics train_flow.py ──post_training──> WeightWatcher ──> MLflow metrics gpu_benchmark.py ──one_shot──> YAML cache ──> MLflow params
Metric Prefix Taxonomy: prof_ — torch.profiler metrics diag_ww_ — WeightWatcher diagnostics diag_ — pre-training checks sys_bench_— GPU benchmark cache params
Parent issue: #643 Sub-issues: #644 (DONE), #645, #646, #647, #648, #649, #650, #651 Total estimated effort: 28 hours (Phase 0 done = 3h saved)

phase.name: PyTorch Profiler Core (Phase 1)
phase.description: Integrate torch.profiler into trainer.py. 7 named regions in training, 4 in validation. Profiler runs for first N epochs via schedule(). Chrome traces exported as gzip-compressed MLflow artifacts. Issues: #645, #646, #647. Effort: 10 hours.

task.description: record_function annotations in trainer.py (6 train + 4 val regions)
torch.profiler.profile context in fit() + TraceHandler + ProfilingSummary
MLflow profiling artifact logging + DuckDB exclusion for prof_* metrics

phase.name: Pre/Post-Training Diagnostics (Phase 2)
phase.description: Pre-training sanity checks (6 checks) + WeightWatcher spectral analysis. Diagnostics ALWAYS run regardless of ProfilingConfig.enabled. Issues: #648, #649. Effort: 6 hours.

task.description: Pre-training sanity checks module (output shape, gradient flow, loss sanity, etc.)
WeightWatcher post-training spectral analysis (diag_ww_ prefix)
Wire pre/post checks into train_flow.py (diagnostics always run)

phase.name: GPU Benchmark Container (Phase 3)
phase.description: Run-once GPU capability benchmarking. YAML cache with per-model throughput, peak VRAM, and feasibility flags. Closes #564. Issues: #650, #651. Effort: 8 hours.

task.description: GPU benchmark script + cache YAML (normalized GPU names, feasibility checks)
Dockerfile.benchmark + compose service
Benchmark cache integration with training flow (sys_bench_ params)

phase.name: Memory Stats + Regression Detection (Phase 4)
phase.description: Per-epoch CUDA memory statistics and performance regression detection. Effort: 4 hours.

task.description: CUDA memory stats per-epoch logging (prof_cuda_peak_allocated_mb, etc.)
Performance regression detection helpers (baseline comparison, GPU matching)

stream.name: S3 Test Suite
stream.description:
Comprehensive S3 storage test suite across all 3 environments. Imported from s3-mounting-testing-and-simulation-plan.md.
Decisions (all ACCEPTED from the plan): D1: Pulumi ComponentResource per provider (UpCloudS3Stack) D2: .env + Docker Compose env injection (maintain current pattern) D3: MinIO in Docker + filesystem fallback D4: Marker-based test gating (@requires_s3_cloud, @requires_s3_local) D5: Shared credentials, separate buckets D6: Implement UpCloud only, design for extension
Total estimated effort: 10-16 hours across 6 phases.

phase.name: Pulumi ComponentResource Refactor (Phase 1)
phase.description: Extract UpCloud S3 resources into ComponentResource. Define S3StackOutputs interface for multi-cloud extensibility.

task.description: Create deployment/pulumi/providers/ with S3StackOutputs dataclass and UpCloudS3Stack(ComponentResource). Refactor __main__.py to dispatch. Verify: pulumi preview shows zero diff (output-preserving refactor).

phase.name: Credential Bridge (Phase 2)
phase.description: Unified S3_* env vars in .env.example + Pulumi-to-env bridge script.

task.description: Add unified S3_ENDPOINT_URL, S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID, S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY to .env.example. Create scripts/pulumi_to_env.sh for automated credential population. Maintain backward compat with DVC_S3_* aliases.

phase.name: Test Markers + Fixtures (Phase 3)
phase.description: Add S3-specific test markers and conftest fixtures for environment detection.

task.description: Add markers: @requires_s3_cloud, @requires_s3_local, @requires_s3_any Implement _s3_cloud_available() and _s3_local_available() detection. Auto-skip with descriptive messages.

phase.name: S3 Connectivity Test Suite (Phase 4)
phase.description: 14 tests across 3 environments: cloud S3, local MinIO, filesystem fallback. Tests: ListBuckets, HeadBucket, PutObject/GetObject, multipart upload, MLflow artifact roundtrip, DVC push/pull, filesystem fallback.

task.description: Create tests/v2/cloud/test_s3_connectivity.py (5 cloud tests) Create tests/v2/integration/test_s3_local.py (5 MinIO tests) Create tests/v2/unit/test_s3_filesystem_fallback.py (2 fallback tests) Create tests/v2/unit/test_s3_env_validation.py (2 unit tests)

phase.name: DVC Remote Unification (Phase 5)
phase.description: Update configure_dvc_remote.py to use unified S3_* vars with DVC_S3_* fallback.

task.description: configure_dvc_remote.py reads S3_* with fallback to DVC_S3_*. Update .dvc/config upcloud remote endpoint template.

phase.name: Documentation + Makefile (Phase 6)
phase.description: Add make test-s3-cloud, test-s3-local, test-s3-all targets. Document S3 setup in cloud-tutorial.md.

task.description: Makefile: test-s3-cloud, test-s3-local, test-s3-all deployment/pulumi/providers/README.md: adding new providers guide

execution_order.description:
Stream 1 (Cloud GPU Training) is P0 and executes FIRST. Within Stream 1, the dependency chain is: S1.0 (push image) → S1.1 (P0 smoke tests) → S1.2 (delete bare-VM) → S1.3 (P1 models)
Streams 2 and 3 are P1 and can execute in PARALLEL after S1.1 completes. Within each stream, phases are sequential (each depends on the previous).
Critical path: S1.0.1 → S1.1.1 → S1.1.2 → S1.1.3 → S1.1.4 Total critical path time: ~2 hours (mostly waiting for RunPod jobs)

step.description: Build + push Docker image to GHCR (THE BLOCKER)
sam3_hybrid + vesselfm smoke tests (can run concurrently ~$0.21)
Verify all 3 smoke test results on cloud MLflow
P1 model configs + profiler annotations + Pulumi refactor (parallel)
MLflow profiling logging + S3 test markers (parallel)
Benchmark Docker + cache integration + S3 docs (parallel)
P1 smoke tests + production training YAMLs (parallel)

cost_model.description: RunPod RTX 4090 spot pricing: ~$0.44/hr. Docker image eliminates uv sync overhead (was ~4 min in old plans). DVC pull is the only setup overhead: ~2-3 min for 4.5 GB dataset.

stream.description: Cloud GPU Training (P0 smoke tests + P1 expansion + production YAMLs)

effort_summary.note: P0 critical path (push image + 3 smoke tests + verify) = ~2 hours

risk.title: Docker image too large for RunPod pull
risk.mitigation: minivess-base is 20.6 GB. RunPod has fast container pull but first launch may take 5-10 min. Subsequent launches use cached layers. If problematic, create a slimmer smoke-test-specific image.

risk.title: UpCloud trial expiration during testing
risk.mitigation: UpCloud trial is EUR 250 / 30 days. Priority is to validate the cloud pipeline (S1.0 + S1.1) immediately. S3 test suite (Stream 3) can be completed after trial if needed — local MinIO covers dev testing.

risk.title: GHCR rate limits for unauthenticated pulls
risk.mitigation: RunPod pulls from GHCR. Unauthenticated pulls have rate limits (100/6h). SkyPilot may need GHCR credentials for private repos. Current image is public — verify with: gh api user/packages/container/minivess-base/versions

risk.title: .env sourcing broken in Makefile
risk.mitigation: The `source .env` command doesn't properly export GHCR_USERNAME in all contexts. The Makefile uses $${ } syntax which may need adjustment. Fallback: use grep-based extraction (confirmed working).

metalearning_corrections.correction: SkyPilot = intercloud broker (NSDI'23), NOT IaC like Pulumi/Terraform. All SkyPilot references in this plan use correct terminology.
Docker is THE execution model, not optional overhead. All setup: sections are DATA ONLY — no apt-get, uv sync, git clone, pip install.
All 9 planning docs were read line-by-line before writing this plan. Invalidated hypotheses are explicitly listed with reasons.
Interactive questions use AskUserQuestion tool, not markdown text dumps.
Infrastructure status verified programmatically, not by asking the user.
